title: Epic 4: Dependency Graph — Context-Aware Work Discovery
description: Agents and developers can establish directional blocking relationships between issues, query the ready queue, find what's blocking a specific task, visualize the full dependency graph, filter and search issues, and view aggregated workspace metrics. Covers FR8, FR9, FR10, FR11, FR12, FR13.

comments.text: Milestone reached: Story 4.1 (Establish and Remove Dependency Relationships) implementation complete. Added --remove flag, WithAuditedTx, WithDeadlockRetry, lexicographic lock ordering, cycle detection, and structured error codes. PR pending review.

comments.message:
✅ Story 4.5 (Filter, Search and View Issues) complete. 

Implemented: list command filtering (--priority, --assignee), enhanced search across issue comments with full-text support.

Completed stories: 4.1 (Dependencies), 4.2 (Ready Queue), 4.3 (Blockers), 4.4 (Visualization), 4.5 (Discovery)

Remaining: 4.6 (Metrics & Stats)
Epic 4 complete. All 6 stories (4.1–4.6) closed. Dependency graph, ready queue, blocker queries, visualization, filtering/search, and workspace metrics all implemented and reviewed.

title: Story 4.1: Establish and Remove Dependency Relationships
description:
As a developer or agent, I want to create directional blocking relationships between issues, so that the system knows which tasks must complete before others can start.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given issues abc123def456 (blocker) and def456abc123 (blocked) both exist, when I run grava dep --add abc123def456 def456abc123, then a row is inserted in the dependencies table
- The operation uses WithDeadlockRetry to handle concurrent dep writes (ADR-H3)
- grava dep --remove abc123def456 def456abc123 deletes the dependency row
- Attempting to create a circular dependency returns CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY error
- grava dep --add on a non-existent issue ID returns ISSUE_NOT_FOUND error

FR Coverage: FR8

comments.text: Session Summary (Story 4.1): Refactored addDependency with WithAuditedTx, WithDeadlockRetry, lexicographic lock ordering (ADR-H3), issue existence validation, cycle detection. Implemented removeDependency with audited transactions. Added --remove flag, --json output, EventDependencyRemove constant. 10 new unit tests, all pass. Commit: 17a2b78

comments.message: Closing — implementation complete, reviewed, and merged.

title: Story 4.2: Query the Ready Queue
description:
As an agent, I want to query the top-priority tasks with no active blockers, so that I can immediately identify what work is actionable without manual triage.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with 10 issues (some blocked, some not), when I run grava ready, then only issues with status NOT IN (done, archived) AND no active (non-done) blockers are returned
- Results ordered by priority (high to low) then by created_at ascending
- grava ready --json returns a JSON array conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava ready --limit 5 returns at most 5 issues
- If all open issues are blocked, returns empty array with exit code 0

FR Coverage: FR9

comments.text: Session Summary: Story 4.2 complete. Added ReadyTaskOutput struct with Assignee field (AC#2), fetchAssignees batch query, fixed empty state to use stderr (AC#3). 8 new unit tests covering independent tasks, blocked-by-done, blocked-by-open, empty state JSON/human, assignee in JSON. Commit: ccfbc79

title: Story 4.3: Query Blockers for a Specific Issue
description:
As a developer or agent, I want to see exactly what upstream issues are blocking a specific task, so that I can resolve blockers in the right order to unblock work.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given issue def456abc123 is blocked by abc123def456 and xyz789abc123, when I run grava blocked def456abc123, then a list showing each blocker (id, title, status, assignee) is returned
- grava blocked def456abc123 --json returns a JSON array conforming to NFR5 schema
- If the issue has no blockers, returns empty array
- grava blocked on a non-existent issue returns ISSUE_NOT_FOUND error

FR Coverage: FR10

comments.message: Implementation complete. Commit: 713348f. All 4 ACs satisfied. Closing.

title: Story 4.4: Visualize the Full Dependency Graph
description:
As a developer or agent, I want to visualize or traverse the overarching dependency structure, so that I can understand the full project topology and identify critical paths.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with interconnected dependency relationships, when I run grava graph, then ASCII-art dependency tree is printed to stdout
- grava graph --format=dot outputs Graphviz DOT-format graph
- grava graph --format=json outputs JSON adjacency list (nodes and edges) conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava graph --root abc123def456 outputs only the subgraph reachable from that issue
- Isolated issues (no deps) appear as standalone nodes, not omitted

FR Coverage: FR11

comments.message: MEDIUM finding fixed: non-existent root node now returns error. Commit: 61813fc. Closing.
Implementation complete. Commit: 389349c. Graph visualization with ASCII, DOT, and JSON formats. Subgraph support via --root flag. All tests passing. Labeled for code review.

[MEDIUM] review 389349c — 1 finding(s)

- pkg/graph/render.go:314-327 (getReachableNodes) — Non-existent root node produces empty/silent failure instead of error. When --root is passed with a node ID that does not exist in the graph, the function silently returns an empty result, producing confusing empty output rather than a clear error message. Fix: Validate root node exists upfront before entering the render pipeline, or return an error from getReachableNodes if startID is not in dag.nodes.

[REVIEW] 389349c — 6 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=1 low=0

Summary: Implemented graph visualization with three rendering formats (ASCII, DOT, JSON) supporting full-graph and subgraph views. Comprehensive test coverage (18 tests). Clean code architecture with proper separation of concerns. Minor validation gap: non-existent root node IDs produce silent empty output instead of errors.

Verdict: APPROVED

title: Story 4.5: Filter, Search and View Issues
description:
As a developer or agent, I want to filter the issue list, search by keyword, and view full details of a single issue, so that I can quickly locate and inspect any work item.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with multiple issues, when I run grava list --status in_progress --assignee agent-01, then only matching issues are returned
- grava search login performs case-insensitive full-text search across title, description, and comments
- grava show abc123def456 displays full issue detail (title, status, priority, assignee, labels, comments, subtasks, dependency info)
- grava show abc123def456 --json returns complete issue object conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava list --json returns JSON array of issue summaries
- All commands return empty results (not errors) when no issues match

FR Coverage: FR12

comments.message: Implementation complete. Commit: 6829910. Labeled for code review.

[LOW] review 6829910 — 1 finding

- pkg/cmd/issues/issues.go:523 — Unused count variable. Minor style cleanup. Consider removing the unused assignment or using the variable for an output line if needed.

[REVIEW] 6829910 — 4 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=0 low=1

Summary: Story 4.5 implementation successfully adds list filtering (--priority, --assignee flags with AND logic) and enhances search with full-text searching across issue comments via LEFT JOIN. All acceptance criteria properly implemented and thoroughly tested with 11 new test cases.

Verdict: APPROVED
✅ Code review finding fixed. Commit: 2ccd4ee34da6b0ee1d3845a97c5381b4e3eef6dc

[MEDIUM] review a27a5b5 — 1 finding

- pkg/cmd/graph/graph.go:700-705 — Search command does not exclude archived issues by default. List command filters status != 'archived' unless --include-archived is set, but search returns archived issues. Dev Notes specify 'Always exclude archived issues by default.' Pre-existing behavior (not a regression), but a spec gap. Fix: add AND i.status != 'archived' to the search WHERE clause.

[LOW] review a27a5b5 — 1 finding

- pkg/cmd/graph/search_test.go:22,59,96,131,168,199 — db.Close() calls missing //nolint:errcheck comments. issues_test.go has them (commit a27a5b5) but search_test.go was missed because macOS sed -i requires sed -i '' for in-place editing. Style inconsistency between the two new test files. Fix: add //nolint:errcheck to all 6 defer db.Close() lines in search_test.go.

[REVIEW] a27a5b5 — 4 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=1 low=1

Summary: Re-review after prior LOW finding fix. Implementation correctly adds list filtering (--priority, --assignee with AND logic) and enhances search with LEFT JOIN to issue_comments. 11 new tests with good coverage. One pre-existing spec gap (search doesn't exclude archived) and one missed nolint annotation in search_test.go.

Verdict: APPROVED

title: Story 4.6: View Workspace Metrics and Stats
description:
As a developer, I want to view aggregated workspace performance and status metrics, so that I can assess project health and identify bottlenecks at a glance.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a workspace with issues in various states, when I run grava stats, then output includes: total issues by status, count of blocked issues, count of stale in_progress issues (no heartbeat >1h), average cycle time for done issues
- grava stats --json returns structured JSON object conforming to NFR5 schema
- grava stats completes in <100ms at 1K issues (Phase 1 baseline)

FR Coverage: FR13

comments.message:
[MEDIUM] review 684f930 — 1 finding

- pkg/cmd/graph/stats_test.go:34-68 — Test helper expectStatsQueries uses loose regex patterns (e.g. 'SELECT COUNT.*status = blocked') instead of regexp.QuoteMeta. Loose patterns can match unintended queries and hide query-order bugs or wrong SQL. Other tests in this package (search_test.go, issues_test.go) use regexp.QuoteMeta for exact matching. Fix: replace pattern strings with regexp.QuoteMeta of the exact expected SQL.

[LOW] review 684f930 — 1 finding

- pkg/cmd/graph/graph.go:944 — 'if stats.AvgCycleTimeMinutes > 0' hides the Avg Cycle Time line when the average genuinely rounds to 0.0 (sub-minute tasks). Unlikely in practice but semantically incorrect — the condition should test whether data was present, not whether the value is non-zero. Fix: track a separate avgMinutes != nil boolean to gate the text output line.

[REVIEW] 684f930 — 2 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=1 low=1

Summary: Adds three new AC-required metrics to grava stats: explicit blocked_count, stale_in_progress_count (COALESCE heartbeat/updated_at >1h), and avg_cycle_time_minutes (TIMESTAMPDIFF on work session columns). All three appear in both text and JSON output. 4 new tests cover text output, JSON schema, zero counts, and missing cycle time data.

Verdict: APPROVED
Both review findings fixed. Commit: a1e0f0c. Ready to close.

title: Fix --last-commit flag in update and expose last_commit in show --json
description: Two bugs fixed: (1) grava update --last-commit fails when used as the only flag because it never registers in changedFields. (2) grava show --json does not include the last_commit field from metadata, making it impossible for skills/agents to retrieve the commit hash programmatically.

title: Story 3.2: Write and Read Wisp Ephemeral State

comments.message:
[CRITICAL] review 44f197a — 2 finding(s)

- **Context Parameter Ignored in wispRead Queries** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:104,117,133) — All read operations use non-context QueryRow() and Query() variants despite ctx parameter being available. Should use QueryRowContext() and QueryContext() to respect cancellation and timeouts. This violates Go best practices and prevents proper timeout/cancellation propagation from CLI context.

- **Type Safety Loss with any Return Type** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:100-101) — wispRead returns (any, error) with two concrete types (*WispEntry or []WispEntry). Call sites use type assertion switch (lines 226-240) without default case. If future code path returns wrong type, causes runtime panic. Fix: Use union type or separate functions for type safety.

[HIGH] review 44f197a — 4 finding(s)

- **Context Not Propagated in Issue Existence Check** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:104-105) — Uses store.QueryRow() instead of store.QueryRowContext(ctx, ...). Inconsistent with wispWrite which uses tx.QueryRowContext(). Should respect context cancellation. Fix: Change to QueryRowContext variant.

- **Context Not Propagated in Single-Key Query** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:117-121) — Uses store.QueryRow() for single-key lookup and store.Query() for multi-entry path. Neither uses context variants. Context cancellation will be ignored. Fix: Use QueryRowContext() and QueryContext().

- **Resource Cleanup Masked by nolint** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:327) — rows.Close() error ignored with //nolint:errcheck. While rows.Close() rarely fails in practice, suppressing the check masks potential resource leaks. Fix: Evaluate if error should be checked or use more targeted lint exception.

- **Missing Test for All-Entries with Non-Existent Issue** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp_test.go) — Test coverage includes single-key ISSUE_NOT_FOUND (line 423-430) but missing test for reading all entries (empty key) from non-existent issue. Fix: Add TestWispRead_AllEntries_IssueNotFound.

[MEDIUM] review 44f197a — 2 finding(s)

- **Error Chain Preservation in errors.Is() Check** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:395-398, 423-426) — Code uses errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) to detect missing entries. This relies on proper error wrapping in gravaerrors.New() to preserve the error chain. If gravaerrors.New() doesn't wrap correctly, errors.Is() will fail silently. Fix: Document error wrapping contract or add explicit error chain preservation if needed.

- **No Validation on Key Length Before Insert** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:341) — Key parameter passed to database without validation. Schema defines key_name VARCHAR(255). Oversized keys will produce raw MySQL error instead of structured GravaError with clear validation message. Fix: Add validation to reject keys exceeding 255 chars with descriptive error before database operation.

[LOW] review 44f197a — 7 finding(s)

- **JSON Output Format Inconsistency** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:298-303, WispEntry struct) — Single-entry write result includes issue_id in JSON output. Single-entry read (WispEntry) and all-entries ([]WispEntry) omit it. Creates inconsistent API surface. Fix: Either add issue_id to read outputs or document the intentional asymmetry.

- **No Protection Against Very Large Value Blob** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:21, migration:008) — Schema defines value TEXT (64KB max). No input validation before storing. Large values can exhaust connection buffer or timeout transaction. Fix: Add size check before insert, e.g. reject values >1MB with VALUE_TOO_LARGE error.

- **Missing Null Check on d.Store Dereference** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:180,216,178) — Commands dereference d.Store without null check. If Deps initialization fails, panics without clear error. Fix: Add defensive nil checks at command entry points or rely on comprehensive test coverage.

- **Inconsistent Error Wrapping in Callback** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:56-62) — Callback errors returned directly; outer errors handled separately. No re-wrapping of outer errors. Pattern is acceptable but inconsistent. Fix: Document error wrapping contract or standardize approach.

- **ALTER TABLE Migration Lacks IF NOT EXISTS** (pkg/migrate/migrations/008_create_wisp_entries.sql:16) — ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN missing IF NOT EXISTS clause. Re-running migration or applying to pre-existing database fails with duplicate column error. Fix: Add IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency.

- **Single vs Multi-Entry Output Format Inconsistency** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go:228-229 vs 236) — Human-readable single-entry output includes issue ID prefix; multi-entry output omits it. JSON output consistent, but text format differs. Fix: Standardize prefix usage or document intentional difference.

- **No Input Validation for Key Length** (pkg/cmd/issues/wisp.go) — Empty keys allowed and written to database. Should enforce minimum length >0. Fix: Add validation to reject empty keys with KEY_REQUIRED error.

[REVIEW] 44f197a — 9 files, critical=2 high=4 medium=2 low=7

Summary: Story 3.2 implements Wisp ephemeral state write/read with correct database schema, audit logging, and comprehensive test coverage. All 8 acceptance criteria are technically satisfied. However, critical architectural issue: wispRead() accepts context parameter but ignores it for all database queries, preventing proper cancellation/timeout propagation — violates Go best practices. Additionally, type-safety issue with any return type and missing input validation on key/value length.

Verdict: CHANGES_REQUESTED

title: Story 5.1: Automatic Master-Root Resolution
Story 5.2: Claim → .worktree/ Provisioning

comments.message: Completed: Added worktree provisioning utilities (CheckWorktreeConflict, ProvisionWorktree, DeleteWorktree) with tests
Completed: Updated claim command with atomic worktree provisioning and rollback logic
Completed: Added integration tests for rollback functionality
Implementation complete. Commit: 04b5d4b. Ready for code review.

[HIGH] review 04b5d4b — 2 finding(s)

- pkg/utils/worktree.go:DeleteWorktree — Returns nil even if git worktree remove or branch deletion fails. For rollback scenarios, silent failures hide state inconsistencies. Consider capturing and returning errors instead of ignoring with underscore.
- pkg/utils/worktree.go:ProvisionWorktree — No stderr/stdout capture from git command. Users won't see git error details, only 'failed to provision worktree'. Reduces debuggability. Consider piping stderr to help troubleshooting.

[MEDIUM] review 04b5d4b — 2 finding(s)

- Tests: Missing end-to-end test for full claim → provision → rollback flow in newClaimCmd. Current tests cover individual functions but not the command handler orchestration. Consider adding integration test with mock DB and git ops.
- pkg/cmd/issues/claim.go:rollbackClaimDB (line 177) — Timeout value (5s) is hardcoded without explanation. Document why 5s was chosen, or consider making it configurable via context or constant.

[LOW] review 04b5d4b — 1 finding(s)

- pkg/cmd/issues/claim.go:newClaimCmd (line 172) — Success message shows worktree path and branch separately. Consider adding explicit mention that the branch name matches the directory name for clarity (e.g., 'branch: grava/<issue-id>'). Current format is clear but could emphasize the relationship.

[REVIEW] 04b5d4b — 4 files, critical=0 high=2 medium=2 low=1

Summary: Solid implementation of worktree provisioning with atomic DB+filesystem operations and rollback. Core logic is correct with good error handling in the command orchestration. Two HIGH findings around error propagation in cleanup functions would improve debuggability and state consistency on failure paths.

Verdict: CHANGES_REQUESTED
Fixed HIGH findings from code review. Commit: d9eb1a5. ProvisionWorktree now captures git stderr; DeleteWorktree now returns errors instead of swallowing them.

Session summary: Implemented worktree provisioning (CheckWorktreeConflict, ProvisionWorktree, DeleteWorktree), atomic claim→provision→rollback in command handler, resolved HIGH code review findings (stderr capture, error propagation). All tests green.

title: Story 5.5: Configure Claude & Git on Init

comments.message: Implementation complete. Commit: dc19c6b. Ready for code review.

[HIGH] review dc19c6b — 1 finding(s)

- pkg/utils/worktree_init.go:92 — Nil map write panic if settings.json contains JSON `null`. After `json.Unmarshal([]byte("null"), &settings)`, settings remains nil; the read at line 87 is safe (nil map read returns zero) but the write at line 92 (`settings["worktree"] = ...`) will panic. Fix: add `if settings == nil { settings = make(map[string]interface{}) }` after the unmarshal block.

[MEDIUM] review dc19c6b — 1 finding(s)

- pkg/cmd/init.go:37 — Git version check runs before worktree detection (step 0a before step 0). Worktree-only init does not use `git worktree remove`, so requiring 2.17+ is slightly over-strict for that path. Low practical impact since worktrees need 2.15+ anyway. Fix: move version check inside the non-worktree branch, after step 0.

[LOW] review dc19c6b — 1 finding(s)

- pkg/utils/worktree_init.go:98 — `json.MarshalIndent` does not preserve original key order. Re-serializing an existing settings.json may reorder keys, creating unnecessary diffs if the file is version-controlled. Fix: use an ordered JSON library or read/write with `json.RawMessage` to preserve order.

[REVIEW] dc19c6b — 5 files, critical=0 high=1 medium=1 low=1

Summary: Adds worktree directory setup, git config injection, Claude settings merge, and Git version pre-flight to grava init. All operations idempotent and non-fatal. Good test coverage (14 new tests). One HIGH: nil map panic on unusual settings.json content.

Verdict: CHANGES_REQUESTED
Fixed HIGH finding (nil map panic). Commit: 89cfa72. Review findings addressed.

Session Summary: Implemented Story 5.5 — Configure Claude & Git on Init. Added 4 new functions (EnsureWorktreeDir, EnsureWorktreeGitignore, SetWorktreeGitConfig, EnsureClaudeWorktreeSettings) + CheckGitVersion preflight. 15 new unit tests, all passing. Fixed HIGH review finding (nil map panic). Docs updated.

title: Story 5.4: Recursive Lifecycle Cleanup

comments.message: Completed: IsWorktreeDirty, RemoveWorktreeOnly, IsInsideClaudeWorktree utilities with tests
Completed: grava close command — dirty check, worktree+branch teardown, Claude symlink cleanup, status→closed via graph
Completed: grava stop now removes worktree directory while keeping branch for resumption
Implementation complete. Commit: bba93db. Ready for code review.

[HIGH] review bba93db — 4 finding(s)

- close.go:63 — Worktree cleanup error swallowed, status still set to closed. If DeleteWorktree fails (git lock, permissions), DB says closed but worktree/branch persist on disk — inconsistent state. Should fail the command or surface partial cleanup prominently.
- close.go:74-83 — Filesystem operations happen before DB update. If SetNodeStatus fails, worktree and branch are already deleted — unrecoverable. Invert order: DB update first (retriable), then filesystem cleanup (idempotent).
- close.go:33 — No issue existence check before filesystem ops. Unlike stop (which queries DB first), close jumps to worktree cleanup. If issue ID is bogus, filesystem ops silently succeed (nothing to clean), then SetNodeStatus fails late. Validate existence upfront.
- stop.go:119 — os.Getwd() failure silently skips worktree cleanup. User sees 'Stopped work on X' success but .worktree/<id> directory remains on disk. Should at minimum log a warning to stderr.

[MEDIUM] review bba93db — 5 finding(s)

- close_test.go:47-64 — TestCloseCmd_CleansUpSymlink tests os.Remove directly, not the close command. It exercises stdlib, not the RunE code path. Should invoke the command or exercise the close logic.
- close_test.go:19-40 — TestCloseCmd_BlocksDirtyWorktree only tests IsWorktreeDirty util, not the command's error return (WORKTREE_DIRTY) or --force bypass. Actual command behavior is untested.
- close.go vs stop.go — stop removes worktree but does not clean up .claude/worktrees/<id> symlink, leaving it dangling. May confuse Claude Code or LinkClaudeWorktree on resume.
- worktree.go:308 — RemoveWorktreeOnly uses --force unconditionally. stop has no dirty check, so it will silently destroy uncommitted work without warning (close guards this, stop does not).
- close.go — Missing --json error path. Errors are returned raw rather than through writeJSONError (which stop uses). Breaks JSON output contract when close fails.

[LOW] review bba93db — 3 finding(s)

- close_test.go:70-88 — setupGitRepo duplicates test helper pattern already in worktree_test.go (runCmd + git init). Could share helpers to avoid drift.
- worktree.go:324-326 — IsInsideClaudeWorktree uses strings.Contains which is loose. Path like /tmp/.claude/worktrees-backup/foo would false-positive. Add leading slash: Contains(path, '/.claude/worktrees/').
- close.go:88 — json.NewEncoder.Encode appends trailing newline, differs from stop's json.MarshalIndent+Fprintln pattern. Minor JSON output format inconsistency.

[REVIEW] bba93db — 6 files, critical=0 high=4 medium=5 low=3

Summary: Adds grava close command with dirty-check guard, worktree+branch teardown, Claude symlink cleanup, and updates grava stop to remove worktree directory on pause. Core functionality is correct and tests pass, but the close command has ordering/consistency issues (filesystem before DB, swallowed cleanup errors, missing JSON error path) and the command-level tests exercise utilities directly rather than the actual command paths.

Verdict: CHANGES_REQUESTED

Story 8.2: Pre-Commit Hook Enforcement
description: Pre-commit Git hook blocks commits to paths held by another agent's exclusive file lease. Checks staged files against active reservations, blocks with structured error on conflict, allows own leases and shared leases through.

comments.message: Implementation complete. Commit: 98f9d13. Ready for code review.

[MEDIUM] review 98f9d13 — 2 finding(s)

- pkg/cmd/reserve/enforce.go:90 — `filepath.Match` does not support recursive globs (`**`). Patterns like `src/**/*.go` silently fail to match. Not a bug for current ACs (which use single-level globs) but a future gotcha if users try doublestar patterns. Fix: document limitation or use a doublestar library.
- pkg/cmd/hook_test.go — No test for AC#3 (own-lease passthrough). SQL filters `agent_id != ?` but no test verifies that a lease held by the committing agent itself is allowed through. Fix: add test with matching actor and lease holder.

[REVIEW] 98f9d13 — 5 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=2 low=0

Summary: Adds pre-commit file reservation enforcement to the hook pipeline. Clean implementation with fail-open semantics, proper DB isolation, and testable injection points. All 6 ACs satisfied. 14 new tests covering pattern matching, conflict blocking, no-conflict passthrough, DB failure, and empty staging.

Verdict: APPROVED

Session Summary: Implemented Story 8.2 Pre-Commit Hook Enforcement. Added CheckStagedConflicts() in reserve pkg with glob matching, wired into runPreCommit with fail-open semantics. 14 new tests. Code review: APPROVED. Docs updated.

Story 10.4: TS-04 & TS-07 — Export/Import Round-Trip and Conflict Detection
description: Implement sandbox scenarios TS-04 (export/import round-trip verifying NFR4 zero-loss handoff) and TS-07 (conflict detection via LWW merge driver).

comments.message: Implementation complete. Commit: 8630316. Ready for code review.

[REVIEW] 8630316 — 4 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=0 low=0

Summary: Two sandbox scenarios implemented. TS-04 validates zero-loss export/import with deps, labels, and comments. TS-07 tests LWW merge driver under 3 conflict patterns (equal timestamp, delete-wins, clear LWW). Both follow existing scenario patterns with proper cleanup. ExportFlatJSONL/ImportFlatJSONL exported as thin wrappers.

title: Story 10.5: Remaining Phase 1 Sandbox Scenarios (TS-03,05,08,09,10)
description: Implement 5 remaining sandbox scenarios: TS-03 (dep graph under load), TS-05 (doctor detection), TS-08 (file reservation enforcement), TS-09 (worktree crash recovery), TS-10 (rapid swarm claims).

comments.message: Session Summary: Implemented 5 remaining sandbox scenarios. All tests pass. Commit: 9a03dc4.

title: Bug: TS-03 missing ReadyQueue result validation (false positive)
description: TS-03 scenario only checks ReadyQueue calls succeed without error but never validates that blocked issues are excluded from results. If ReadyQueue returned ALL issues ignoring dependencies, the test still passes. Need to verify only unblocked leaf issues appear.

title: Bug: TS-04 does not verify labels/comments survive round-trip
description: TS-04 export/import round-trip verifies issues and dependencies survive but never checks that labels and comments are present after import. If importFlatJSONL silently dropped labels/comments, the test would still pass. Add post-import queries for issue_labels and issue_comments.

title: Bug: Dolt server binds 0.0.0.0 — unauthenticated DB exposed to network
description: In init.go line 151, Dolt server starts with --host 0.0.0.0, binding to all interfaces. Combined with root user and no password, any machine on the network can connect and read/write all data. Should bind to 127.0.0.1 (localhost only).

title: Bug: matchPattern does not support ** recursive globs
description: enforce.go matchPattern uses filepath.Match which does not support ** recursive glob. Patterns like pkg/cmd/**/*.go only match one directory level, silently failing to protect deeply nested files. Need doublestar library or manual ** expansion.

title: Bug: unknown actor default bypasses reservation enforcement
description: hook.go defaults actor to 'unknown' when unconfigured. Two agents both using 'unknown' bypass each other's leases because SQL filters agent_id != 'unknown' excludes their own lease — treating them as the same agent. Need unique fallback or skip actor filter when unknown.

title: Bug: ExpiresTS format shows literal UTC regardless of actual timezone
description: hook.go line 377 uses Format('2006-01-02 15:04:05 UTC') which outputs literal 'UTC' text regardless of the time.Time's actual timezone. If DB returns local time, the display is misleading. Fix: use .UTC().Format() or time.RFC3339.

title: Bug: EnsureWorktreeDir does not check if .worktree is a file vs directory
description: worktree_init.go EnsureWorktreeDir uses os.Stat to check existence but does not verify IsDir(). If a regular file named .worktree exists, the function returns (false, nil) and subsequent worktree operations fail with confusing errors.

title: Bug: TS-05 deterministic reservation ID causes collision across runs
description: ts05_doctor.go truncates tag to 10 chars (tag[:10]) for reservation ID. The first 10 chars only change every ~28 hours, causing identical IDs across runs within that window. Use full tag or generate proper unique ID.

title: Bug: TS-10 weak double-claim assertion masks per-issue violations
description: ts10_swarm_claims.go checks aggregate success count <= issueCount but does not verify per-issue uniqueness. If ClaimIssue has a TOCTOU bug where two agents both succeed on the same issue, the aggregate check might still pass. Need per-issue tracking.

title: Bug: importFlatJSONL overwrite=true does not remove stale labels/deps
description: sync.go importFlatJSONL with overwrite=true uses INSERT IGNORE for labels and ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE for issues, but never DELETEs labels/deps that exist in DB but are absent from the imported record. Export-delete-import is not a true round-trip for label removals.

title: Bug: Partial corrupt file left on disk after export error
description: sync.go newExportCmd creates the output file with os.Create, then writes. If exportFlatJSONL fails partway through, the partial file is left on disk. Next merge driver or export may read corrupt/partial data. Need to remove partial file on error.

title: Bug: docs/** fails to match docs itself (zero-segment)
description: enforce.go:122 — Pattern docs/** with path docs returns false because HasPrefix(docs, docs/) is false. ** should match zero segments.

title: Bug: shared lease bypasses conflict check against exclusive leases
description: reserve.go:86 — DeclareReservation gates findConflict behind 'if p.Exclusive'. A shared lease request skips conflict detection entirely, so an agent can obtain a shared lease on a path exclusively held by another agent.

title: Bug: duplicate dead-code error check in enforce.go
description: enforce.go:48-51 — Duplicate if err != nil check after QueryContext. Second check is unreachable dead code.

title: Bug: findConflict uses exact string match not glob overlap
description: reserve.go:122 — SQL uses path_pattern = ? (exact match). Exclusive lease on src/**/*.go won't conflict with request for src/cmd/*.go. Two agents can hold exclusive leases on overlapping paths.

title: Bug: CheckStagedConflicts has zero test coverage
description: enforce_test.go only tests matchPattern. The primary exported function CheckStagedConflicts (query building, DB scanning, actor filtering) has no tests.

title: Bug: reserve tests missing WithArgs on mock expectations
description: reserve_test.go — Multiple mock expectations lack WithArgs, so tests pass even if wrong arguments are sent to SQL queries.

title: Bug: TOCTOU race in DeclareReservation (no transaction)
description: reserve.go:86-107 — Conflict check and INSERT are separate SQL ops with no transaction. Two agents can pass the check simultaneously and both insert exclusive leases for the same pattern.

title: Bug: multiple ** in glob pattern silently broken
description: enforce.go:114 — SplitN(pattern, **, 2) only handles first **. Pattern src/**/cmd/**/*.go passes suffix with ** to filepath.Match which can't handle it.

title: Bug: time.Until uses local clock vs DB server clock
description: reserve.go:301 — reserve --list computes EXPIRES_IN via time.Until(r.ExpiresTS) comparing DB-generated timestamp against local Go clock. Clock skew causes wrong display.

title: Remove legacy ExportItem wrapped import path
description: The legacy import path (grava import --file) accepts a wrapped format ({"type":"issue","data":{...}}) that no code in the codebase produces. Keeping an importer for a dead format is a trap — users who manually edit issues.jsonl in that format get cryptic pre-commit failures. Remove ExportItem, IssueExportData, DependencyExportData structs and importIssues() function, plus the --file flag branch.

title: ValidateJSONL gives cryptic error on legacy wrapped format
description: When issues.jsonl contains the legacy wrapped format, ValidateJSONL reports 'missing required field id' which gives no clue about the actual problem. Should detect the wrapped format pattern and return an actionable message like: 'file uses legacy wrapped format; run grava export to regenerate'.

title: grava import does not auto-export issues.jsonl after DB mutation
description: After a successful positional grava import, the issues.jsonl file on disk may be stale or in wrong format. Should auto-run exportFlatJSONL after import to keep the file in sync with what pre-commit validates.
